title: What Would Happen If A METEOR Hit Earth?
summary: This interview segment discusses the dangers of meteor impacts on Earth, referencing the Shoemaker-Levy 9 comet collision with Jupiter in 1994 and the 1908 Tunguska event as examples of catastrophic potential from cosmic objects.

events.description: Comet Shoemaker-Levy 9 collided with Jupiter, creating an impact equivalent to 6 million megatons of TNT
Tunguska event occurred on June 30th - an air burst explosion that flattened 2,000 square miles of trees in Siberia

key_claims.claim: A cosmic impact event posed danger to humanity 12,800 years ago and remains a threat today

Comet Shoemaker-Levy 9 was only a little over half a mile wide but its impact on Jupiter was equivalent to 6 million megatons of TNT, or 600 times the destructive force of Earth's entire nuclear arsenal
The Tunguska event was an air burst that flattened 2,000 square miles of trees but killed nobody because it occurred over an uninhabited area of Siberia
The Tunguska event occurring on June 30th strongly suggests it was part of the Taurid meteor stream, as that is the peak of the Taurids
